A WATER SPOUT MOVED ON SHORE NEAR THE 900 BLOCK OF NORTH OCEAN BLVD. THE 15 YARD WIDE FUNNEL PROCEEDED ONSHORE FOR ABOUT 100 YARDS BEFORE DISSIPATING, AND CAUSED NO APPARENT DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES. HOWEVER BEACH CHAIRS AND UMBRELLAS WERE TOSSED INTO THE AIR. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF-0 WITH WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED AT NO MORE THAN 65 MPH.

A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF BICAYNE DRIVE AT PAGES CREEK. THE TORNADO MOVED NW FOR ABOUT 2 MINUTES AND SNAPPED A COUPLE DOZEN TREES UP TO 14 IN DIAMETER, UPROOTED SEVERAL LARGE TREES, AND CAUSED SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME ON BISCAYNE DRIVE. THE TORNADO ENDED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BAYSHORE DRIVE AND BISCAYNE DRIVE.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BRIEFLY ALONG BLUE CLAY ROAD NORTH OF I-140 AND WEST OF NORTH COLLEGE ROAD. SEVERAL HARDWOOD TREES UP TO 10 INCHES IN DIAMETER WERE SNAPPED OFF APPROXIMATELY 15 FEET OFF THE GROUND.

TORNADO CAUSED DAMAGE NEAR EMPIE PARK, ACROSS FOREST HILLS, TO THE LOVE GROVE COMMUNITY. EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE TO AROUND 100 TREES AROUND A 3 MILE PATH. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 85 TO 95 MPH. FURTHER DETAILS WILL BE INCLUDED IN A PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT.
APPROXIMATELY HALF A DOZEN PINE AND OAK TREES UP TO 14 INCHES IN DIAMETER WERE SNAPPED, SOME WERE TWISTED, ON RIVER VISTA DRIVE. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED 95 MPH EF1.

A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN NORTHERN HORRY COUNTY CAUSING SPOTTY DAMAGE TO PINE TREES AND MINOR ROOF DAMAGE.

NWS STORM SURVEY ASSESSED DAMAGE FROM A POSSIBLE TORNADO NEAR TABOR CITY, NC AND FOUND EVIDENCE OF A 105 MPH, EF1 TORNADO. ABOUNT 50 LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED; SOME OF THE LARGER TREES WERE APPROXIMATELY 22 IN DIAMETER. MINOR ROOF DAMAGE WAS FOUND ON A COUPLE OF HOUSES ALONG WITH DESTROYED CARPORTS AND MODERATE ROOF DAMAGE TO A WELDING SHOP. RADAR INDICATED THE TIME OF THE TORNADO WAS 10:38 PM. MORE DETAILS WILL BE PROVIDED IN AN UPCOMING PNS.
